title: Lesle Andreya
content:

|birth=[[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]]<ref name="Date: Birth">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is set three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which the ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], meaning ''Wanted by Cracken'' is set in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Since Lesle Andreya is stated to be twenty-eight years old in her entry, she was born in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]].</ref>

|affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]<ref name="WBC" />
**[[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]]<ref name="WBC" />
*[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]<ref name="Defection">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the formation of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], and [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is set three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, placing it in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. In ''Wanted by Cracken'', Lesle Andreya has shifted allegiance to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] "in the past few [[Standard month/Legends|months]]." As the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] had reformed into the New Republic by this [[Time/Legends|time]], she must have continued to serve under the new government's [[New Republic Intelligence Service|Intelligence office]].</ref>
**[[New Republic Intelligence Service]]<ref name="Defection" />
|masters=
|apprentices=
}}
{{Quote|Lesle Andreya was trained by some of the best espionage experts then serving the Rebellion. She knows all the "by the book" tricks, so those hunting her will be forced to improvise. Although she is not violent by nature, there is no telling how she will react if backed into a corner.|General Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WBC" />}}
'''Lesle Andreya''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Neree]]. One of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion's]] top [[Agent/Legends|agents]], she prevented several [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] ambushes on her fellow Rebels. She continued to serve under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken's]] leadership when the Rebellion matured into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] before eventually turning traitor in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].

At that [[Time/Legends|time]], she [[Thief/Legends|stole]] [[Computer/Legends|computer]] files and fled to the planet [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]]. The New Republic believed she then traveled to a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] under Imperial control intending to [[Credit chip/Legends|sell]] the plans to the highest bidder. Cracken wrote about Andreya in his dossier, ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', noting her great skill and cautioning agents to be careful and potentially start their search in the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine's]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Andreya made off with a number of sensitive computer files containing vital defense plans and vanished in the vicinity of Nal Hutta. It is believed she may have obtained transport from there to a sector that remains in Imperial control.|General Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WBC" />}}
Lesle Andreya, who hailed from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Neree]], was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|woman]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]]<ref name="WBC" /> in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]].<ref name="Date: Birth" /> She would serve the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as an [[Agent/Legends|agent]] for [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] under the leadership of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the Alliance and the regime of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Rebellion's best espionage experts trained her, and she knew all the "by the book" tricks. During her work with the Alliance, she provided information that allowed Alliance forces to avoid Imperial ambushes.<ref name="WBC" /> When the Alliance [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|reorganized]] into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Andreya continued to work for the fledgling government.<ref name="Defection" />

Around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="Defection" /> Andreya's loyalties shifted, and she began actively working against the Alliance's successor state; Andreya [[Thief/Legends|stole]] sensitive [[Computer/Legends|computer]] files containing defense plans and disappeared in the region surrounding [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]],<ref name="WBC" /> the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="SWRC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> The [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] believed that she obtained [[Transport/Legends|transport]] from there to an Imperial-controlled [[Sector/Legends|sector]], where they believed she was planning to sell the information for as many [[Credit chip/Legends|credits]] as possible. As such, she was wanted by the New Republic for the charges of bribery, espionage, extortion, sabotage, theft, and treason, prompting the government to post a reward of fifteen thousand credits for her capture.<ref name="WBC" />

[[File:Mos_Eisley.png|thumb|left|250px|Mos Eisley, where it was believed Andreya's family resided.]]
General Cracken, as leader of the New Republic Intelligence Service, included her in his dossier of persons of interest, ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', in a chapter dedicated to [[Spy/Legends|spies]] and informants. The dossier included information on her background and noted that the New Republic was very interested in her motivations for stealing the data as well as recovering the data itself. Cracken also left an additional note on the file, cautioning New Republic agents to be careful of her due to her great skill and training, even if she was trapped. He also recommended that agents start in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] due to the Intelligence Service's belief that she had family located there.<ref name="WBC" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Not even her former employers in the Rebellion know what Andreya truly looks like. She speaks with a trace of a Neree accent.|General Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WBC" />}}
Andreya was a [[Color/Legends|light]]-skinned woman who stood 1.6 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and spent enough time on Neree that she spoke with a trace of a Neree accent. The agent was not known to use methods of violence—she instead preferred to rely on her persuasion skills. Although she had been a skilled agent for the Rebellion and prevented Alliance [[Death/Legends|losses]] from planned Imperial ambushes, she defected for reasons unknown to the New Republic, bringing her loyalty and motivations into question.<ref name="WBC" />

==Skills and abilities==
{{Quote|Lesle Andreya was, at one time, one of the top intelligence agents in the employ of the Rebellion.|General Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WBC" />}}
As an extremely talented spy, Andreya was talented in many skills needed for espionage, such as pick-pocketing, forgery, demolitions, and stealth, to the extent that her former employers did not know her true appearance. She was also quite knowledgeable about the [[Tatoo system/Legends|star system]] of the planet Tatooine.<ref name="WBC" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|She is believed to be armed, but is not known for violence, preferring to use her powers of persuasion to achieve her ends.|General Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WBC" />}}
To conceal her identity, even from her former Rebellion handlers, Andreya dressed in full-body [[Robe/Legends|robes]] that revealed only her [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. [[Limb/Legends|Shin]]-length footwear protected her feet while she was armed with a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]], [[Knife/Legends|knife]], and several [[Fragmentation grenade/Legends|fragmentation grenades]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:LeiaAndreya.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The original concept art]]
Lesle Andreya was featured with her own entry in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a [[1993]] sourcebook written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The image used to depict Andreya<ref name="WBC" /> was initially a concept illustration for [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] for a deleted [[Desert/Legends|desert]] scene in the [[1983]] [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. The artwork was made by [[Nilo Rodis-Jamero]] around [[1981]] and was colored with blue eyes and a brown cloak.<ref name="SWC">''[[Star Wars Costumes: The Original Trilogy]]''</ref> In contrast, the ''Wanted by Cracken'' image was in black and white and attributed to the [[Lucasfilm Archives]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Sources==
